codingTopics: Vectorized columnar scan and aggregation over integer arrays
Cache-aware hash table for GROUP BY on numbers
Merge many sorted data parts (merge, heap)
Sliding-window time-series aggregation over a log stream
systemDesignTopics: Design a columnar storage engine with vectorized query execution for analytical workloads
Design a distributed query engine that shards data across nodes with shard-aware planning
Design a real-time materialized-view maintenance system over a high-ingest event stream
Design a MergeTree-style background compaction system for immutable data parts

coding: Given a column of integers stored in blocks, compute a rolling aggregate (sum/avg over the last k rows) in a single vectorized pass. Discuss cache locality and complexity.
Merge k sorted data parts (each a sorted run of primary-key values) into one sorted output stream, as MergeTree compaction does. Use a heap and analyze complexity.
Build a cache-aware hash table for GROUP BY aggregation over billions of rows; handle collisions and resizing without thrashing the CPU cache.
Given a stream of timestamped events, maintain a rolling count per time bucket for a real-time analytics dashboard. Optimize for high ingest throughput.
Decode a run-length + delta encoded integer column back to raw values using bit operations; handle block-boundary edge cases.
behavioral: Tell me about a time you owned a performance problem end-to-end, from profiling to shipping the fix.
Describe a project where you had to reason far below your usual abstraction layer (CPU cache, memory layout, disk).
Tell me about a technical tradeoff you made between correctness and throughput.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate about an architecture decision and how it resolved.
systemDesign: Design ClickHouse Cloud's separation of storage and compute so many stateless query nodes read from shared object storage. Cover caching, consistency, and cold-start latency.
Design a distributed columnar database that ingests 10M rows/sec and answers analytical queries over trillions of rows. Cover sharding, replication, and query planning.
Design a real-time materialized view that keeps aggregates fresh as new data parts land, without rescanning history.
values: ClickHouse is open source with a large community. Tell me about a time you engaged with or contributed to an open-source project.
Why does query performance matter to you, and where have you obsessed over milliseconds?
How do you balance shipping fast with maintaining a codebase thousands of companies depend on?
other: Do I have to know C++ to interview at ClickHouse?
How does ClickHouse compare to Snowflake or Databricks?

rawSummary:
ClickHouse Interview Guide (2026): Analytical Database Engineering
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

ClickHouse
clickhouse.com ↗

ClickHouse Inc. is the company behind the open-source columnar OLAP database that has become the de facto standard for real-time analytics — Cloudflare, Uber, eBay, and most ad-tech runs on it. The interview is C++-heavy and rewards engineers who genuinely care about cache lines, SIMD, and microsecond-level performance.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute technical phone (C++ + DSA) → onsite loop of 5 rounds: 2 coding (often involving low-level performance), 1 system design (always database internals), 1 past-project deep dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–5 weeks. Bar is high.

What they actually ask

- Design a columnar storage engine with vectorized execution

- Implement a hash table optimized for analytical queries

- Design a distributed query engine with shard-aware planning

- Coding: C++ template metaprogramming, SIMD intrinsics, cache-aware algorithms

- Past-project deep dive: must demonstrate genuine performance work

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $200K–$250K total

- Senior SE: $280K–$380K

- Staff: $420K–$560K

- Principal: $600K–$800K

Prep priorities

- Be deeply fluent in modern C++ (C++17 and later, templates, RAII, move semantics)

- Read the ClickHouse codebase — yes, parts of it. Understand the storage format and query execution

- Brush up on database internals: query planning, vectorized execution, columnar formats (Parquet, ORC)

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I have to know C++ to interview at ClickHouse?

For the core engine, yes. Cloud platform roles accept Go and Python, but the bar for systems engineers is C++.

Is ClickHouse remote-friendly?

Distributed-first across Europe and US. The engineering team has historical concentration in the EU.

How does ClickHouse compare to Snowflake or Databricks?

Smaller team, deeper systems work, lower comp than peak Snowflake/Databricks but with significant equity upside post-funding.
